name: Qiagen N.V.

profile.description: Qiagen N.V. is a global leader in Sample-to-Insight molecular diagnostics, providing technologies and consumables for extracting and analyzing DNA, RNA, and proteins. Key franchises include Sample Technologies, QuantiFERON (latent tuberculosis testing), QIAstat-Dx (syndromic testing), QIAcuity (digital PCR), and QIAGEN Digital Insights (AI-driven bioinformatics).

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Innovation Track Record (2/3): Successful pivot from broad life sciences toward five focused 'growth pillars' that delivered $1.49B (+8% CER) in 2025[1]; Genoox/Franklin acquisition in 2025 added AI-supported genetic interpretation[2]. AI Deployment with Measurable Results (2/2): Roadmap to launch 14 AI-enabled software products by 2028 with concrete 2026 priorities including agentic AI decision support and multilingual clinical reporting; QDI franchise generating real revenue via Franklin platform[2]. Market Share Leadership (2/2): QuantiFERON is the dominant latent TB test globally, +10% CER in 2025 with continued share gain from skin tests[1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Specific 14-product AI roadmap with 2028 timeline, but most products do not yet have revenue visibility[2]. R&D Intensity (0/1): At industry average for diagnostics, not standout.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): 'We leave no one behind' is a distinctive mission tied to bridging health disparities globally; CEO Bernard articulates the cause consistently[2]. Specific commitment to becoming 'the most digital and AI-driven company in our industry'[2]. Community Impact (1/2): Aligned with global health equity through diagnostic access programs but specific dollar amounts not prominent. Employee Retention (2/2): Bernard has been CEO since 2020 with stable executive committee; 6,000 employees globally with multi-decade operating history; cultural emphasis that 'how teams work is just as important as what they achieve'[2]. Insider Buying (0/2): No meaningful insider buying signal from searches; Deutsche Bank upgrade after 30% stock decline suggests fundamental opportunity but no insider conviction signal[3].

infrastructure.label: DEVELOPING
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically calculated. D/E 48.6% scores 1.0 (below 70% target). ROE 11.6% scores 0.77. Revenue Growth YoY 3.7% scores 0.79 (between -20% floor and 10% target). Revenue Growth 5yr CAGR ~5% scores 0.67. Forward P/E 15.2 scores 1.0 (below 20). Profit Margin 20.3% scores 1.0 (above 20% target). Forward PEG ~1.5 scores 0.75 (between 1.0 and 3.0). Weighted: D/E 1.0 (1x) + ROE 0.77 (2x=1.54) + RevYoY 0.79 (1x) + Rev5yr 0.67 (2x=1.34) + FwdPE 1.0 (1x) + Margin 1.0 (1x) + FwdPEG 0.75 (2x=1.50) = 8.17 / 10 = 8.2 -> rounds down considering organic growth deceleration to 6. Liquidity: 2.43M x $41.91 = $101.9M passes liquidity floor.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): Qiagen's core business — physical sample preparation kits, instruments (QIAstat-Dx, QIAcuity), regulated IVD diagnostics — is structurally protected from AI substitution. AI cannot replace the wet-lab chemistry and physical instruments needed to extract DNA/RNA. QuantiFERON is the standard-of-care for latent TB testing and continues to gain share against skin tests[1]. AI is a tailwind on the Digital Insights side, where Qiagen is an active builder rather than a target. AI Opportunity (1/1): QDI franchise generating revenue via Franklin platform with 14 AI products planned by 2028; clear AI revenue trajectory[2].

infinite_game.summary: Qiagen demonstrates strong infinite-game characteristics: a distinctive 'leave no one behind' Just Cause, multi-decade focus on Sample-to-Insight, and a willingness to invest heavily in AI software despite near-term margin pressure. CEO transition language and back-end-loaded 2026 execution risk temper the score.

just_cause.analysis: Genuine cause, partially lived (2 pts). 'We leave no one behind' connects diagnostics work to global health equity in a distinctive way; Bernard cites it consistently and ties strategy back to it[2]. Validated by QuantiFERON's role in TB elimination programs.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Some courageous decisions (1 pt). Heavy AI/digital investment (14-product roadmap by 2028, Genoox/Franklin acquisition) is a long-duration bet[2]. Walked away from the proposed Thermo Fisher acquisition in 2020 to pursue independent strategy.

trusting_teams.analysis: High-trust organization (2 pts). Bernard emphasizes 'how teams work is just as important as what they achieve' and has built stable executive continuity since 2020[2]. Multi-decade German/Dutch operating culture with strong scientific roots.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Some flexibility (1 pt). Pivoted from broad sample prep to focused growth pillars and added a Digital Insights software layer via Genoox[1][2]. Not fully transformative — still primarily a wet-lab company.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Finite competitive mindset (0 pt). Bernard frames competition vs. Thermo Fisher and Roche around share gains; no explicit acknowledgment of competitors as motivational worthy rivals.

investment_thesis: Qiagen is a global Sample-to-Insight molecular diagnostics leader with $2.09B in 2025 sales (+5% CER) and adjusted EPS of $2.40 (above the $2.28 initial outlook)[1]. The five 'growth pillars' (Sample Technologies, QuantiFERON, QIAstat-Dx, QIAcuity, QDI) generated $1.49B (+8% CER) in 2025 and are targeted to reach $2B by 2028. CEO Thierry Bernard has positioned Qiagen as 'the most digital and AI-driven company in our industry' with a roadmap to launch 14 AI-enabled software products by 2028, including agentic AI decision support and multilingual clinical reporting via the Franklin platform acquired with Genoox in 2025[2]. Forward P/E of 15.2, profit margin of 20.3%, and Deutsche Bank upgrade to Buy after a 30% decline suggest the stock is oversold relative to fundamentals[3].
thesis_continued: However, top-line growth is only 5% CER — well below the medical diagnostics peer growth rates of 8-12% — and the 2026 outlook is back-end-loaded, raising execution risk[3]. Q4 2025 net sales were up only 1% CER, and the stock has been in a multi-month drawdown reflecting investor concern about the pace of the AI/digital transformation. Faith alignment is misaligned at -49 due to LGBT activism. Bear case: AI software roadmap fails to translate to meaningful revenue, QuantiFERON growth decelerates, and the Sample Technologies franchise loses share to lower-cost competitors.
key_catalysts: 14 AI-enabled software product launches by 2028 with 2026 priorities including agentic AI decision support
QuantiFERON +10% CER growth and continued conversion from skin tests
Path to $2B in growth pillar sales by 2028 (from $1.49B in 2025)
key_risks: Top-line growth of only 5% CER lags medical diagnostics peers by 3-7 points
2026 outlook is back-end-loaded creating H1 disappointment risk
Faith alignment misaligned at -49 due to LGBT activism positioning
sources: Qiagen Q4 and Full Year 2025 Earnings Release, February 2026
Qiagen CEO Thierry Bernard 2026 growth priorities and AI strategy commentary, accessed April 2026
Deutsche Bank Qiagen upgrade to Buy commentary, March 2026
